title: Protocol Outcomes, Layers, Standards, and Evidence · Execution Trace: Outcome Evidence Contract
summary: A team says it built HTTP from scratch because one request works, but it cannot separate application semantics, wire syntax, transport behavior, implementation policy, or user and operator evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the outcome evidence contract increment in protocol outcome, layer, authority, and evidence charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define user, client, operator, security, accessibility, interoperability, and recovery evidence for a protocol implementation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Outcome Evidence Contract · Theory Outcome Evidence Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 4936 in a municipal gateway receiving one-byte TCP fragments. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate model, wire, state, and authorized live-transfer evidence; preserve this operating constraint: one parser state transition may consume bytes only after its full precondition holds; then truncate one content octet and classify incomplete framing without connection reuse. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Define user, client, operator, security, accessibility, interoperability, and recovery evidence for a protocol implementation.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Outcome Evidence Contract contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Outcome Evidence Contract · Theory Outcome Evidence Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define user, client, operator, security, accessibility, interoperability, and recovery evidence for a protocol implementation. For Predict Outcome Evidence Contract · Theory Outcome Evidence Contract, keep Outcome Evidence Contract connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.title: Build Outcome Evidence Contract evidence · Theory Outcome Evidence Contract
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: httpproto-outcome-evidence-contract-9etwsc" that uses case 9724 to separate model, wire, state, and authorized live-transfer evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets or listeners, negotiate TLS, contact networks or proxies, capture packets, read host state, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the named Go 1.26 protocol transfer gates.

steps.title: Defend the Outcome Evidence Contract model · Theory Outcome Evidence Contract
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Outcome Evidence Contract: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory protocol team handles case 4942 in a multi-tenant intake service near its connection and memory ceilings. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate model, wire, state, and authorized live-transfer evidence; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then force a short write and retain the exact transmitted prefix plus remaining count. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Define user, client, operator, security, accessibility, interoperability, and recovery evidence for a protocol implementation.
